title: Net Worth Calculator: Track Your Financial Health
description: Surprising truth about wealth: Your actual net worth might be shockingly different than you think.

schemas.headline: Net Worth Calculator: Track Your Financial Health
schemas.description: Surprising truth about wealth: Your actual net worth might be shockingly different than you think. Discover the one hidden metric that decides when you can actually retire...

acceptedAnswer.text: Your net worth is the total value of all your assets (what you own) minus the total of all your liabilities (what you owe). It is the most accurate measure of your true financial health and wealth accumulation.

To calculate net worth, list all your assets such as cash, investments, real estate, and valuable personal property. Then list all your liabilities like mortgages, student loans, credit card debt, and auto loans. Subtract the total liabilities from the total assets.

mainEntity.name: Why use a net worth calculator?

acceptedAnswer.text: A net worth calculator simplifies tracking your financial progress over time, ensuring you don't overlook common assets or liabilities. It provides a quick snapshot of your financial health to help you make informed decisions.

mainEntity.name: What is a good net worth by age?

acceptedAnswer.text: A common rule of thumb for a suggested net worth is: (Age x Pre-tax Income) / 10. However, medians vary widely by age group, typically increasing as individuals advance in their careers and pay down debt.

mainEntity.name: Should I include my house in my net worth calculator?

acceptedAnswer.text: Yes, you should include the current market value of your house as an asset, and the remaining mortgage balance as a liability. The difference between the two is your home equity, which directly contributes to your net worth.

schemas.name: Net Worth Calculator
schemas.description: Calculate your net worth by tracking assets and liabilities. See your asset allocation and compare with age-based median net worth.
